patient.query: I guess it's just... I often feel like I'm just a burden, you know? Like, whenever things get tough, I can't shake the feeling that maybe they would have been better off without me. It’s like I'm always waiting for someone to see that I'm not really worth much, or that I just take more than I give.

automatic_thoughts: they would be better off without me
I take more than I give

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: It usually hits me in social situations, like when I'm with friends or family, and I see everyone else having a good time. I just feel out of place, like I’m not contributing anything meaningful. And then I start thinking about all the times I’ve needed help or support, and it feels like I’m just draining everyone around me. It’s hard not to feel like I’m more trouble than I’m worth.

automatic_thoughts: I'm more trouble than I'm worth

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I get that it's common, but it feels so personal to me. When I'm in those moments, I can't help but think that if I just disappeared, no one would really miss me. It’s hard to see my own value, even when I know people are trying to help. I guess I just feel really stuck in that mindset.

I can't see my own value

patient.query: I don’t know... I guess I just think about how easy it is for people to move on when I’m not around. Like, life goes on, and they all have their own lives to focus on. It feels like I'm just kind of an extra in their story. I wish I could believe that my presence matters more, but it’s hard when I can’t seem to shake that feeling.

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're having a hard time feeling significant when others seem to move on without you. Have you ever considered what a trusted friend might think about your role in their life? It could be helpful to explore that perspective."

patient.query: I mean, it’s just the little things, you know? Like when I text someone and they don't respond, or when I’m the one left out of plans. It’s like... those moments feel really loud to me, and they reinforce that maybe I’m not important. I get that it might not be the full picture, but it just really sticks with me.
